security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill provides documentation and CLI command references for Nest.js development - standard patterns for a framework expert skill. No malicious code execution, credential exfiltration, or cryptographic vulnerabilities exist. External commands are documented CLI tools, network URLs are official documentation links, and environment variable references are legitimate configuration guidance.

critical_findings.title: False Positive: Dangerous Combination Heuristic
critical_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Code execution + Network + Credential access' as dangerous. This is a false positive. The skill documents standard development workflows: CLI commands (nest generate), documentation URLs, and JWT_SECRET environment variable configuration - all legitimate Nest.js development patterns.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: This skill provides documentation for Nest.js development workflows. The combination flagged is standard developer tooling - not malicious behavior.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged lines as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. Upon review: lines contain YAML frontmatter, forwardRef() for DI, and TypeORM decorators. No cryptographic code exists.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual code review confirms no cryptographic algorithms are present - these are framework-specific patterns.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Sensitive Data Exposure
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged JWT_SECRET environment variable as sensitive exposure. This is legitimate authentication configuration guidance - standard practice for secure JWT implementation.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documenting JWT_SECRET configuration is expected guidance for Nest.js authentication - not credential exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged shell backticks as 'external_commands'. These are documentation showing CLI commands (nest generate, npm run build, grep, find) - not actual code execution. This is standard developer documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks document Nest.js CLI commands that developers run - not code execution by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Access (Hardcoded URLs)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged hardcoded URLs. These are official documentation links to Nest.js, Jest, TypeORM, and Passport.js - legitimate reference material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to official documentation sites - not external data exfiltration endpoints.

content.value_statement: NestJS developers face complex architectural challenges including dependency injection, module organization, and authentication setup. This skill provides expert guidance on NestJS patterns, testing strategies, and common issue resolution.
actual_capabilities: Diagnose NestJS dependency injection issues and circular dependency problems
Guide module architecture decisions and provider configuration
Implement authentication with Passport.js and JWT strategies
Set up testing with Jest including unit, integration, and e2e tests
Configure TypeORM entities, relationships, and migrations
Debug common NestJS errors from GitHub issues and Stack Overflow
limitations: Does not write complete applications - provides guidance and code patterns
Cannot access external APIs or run live database queries
Limited to NestJS framework - not a general-purpose coding assistant

use_cases.title: Fix NestJS Dependency Injection Errors
use_cases.description: Resolve 'Cannot resolve dependencies' errors by identifying missing providers, incorrect module exports, or circular dependencies.

use_cases.title: Set Up JWT Authentication
use_cases.description: Configure Passport.js strategies with JWT, proper guard implementation, and token validation.

use_cases.title: Write Comprehensive Tests
use_cases.description: Create unit tests with mocks, integration tests with test database, and e2e tests with Supertest.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Circular Dependency
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm getting 'Circular dependency detected' error in my NestJS application between UserService and AuthService. Help me resolve this using forwardRef() or refactor the architecture.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up JWT Auth
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to implement JWT authentication in my NestJS app with Passport.js. Show me how to configure the JwtStrategy, JwtModule, and AuthGuard.

prompt_templates.title: Write Unit Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write unit tests for my UserService using Jest. It depends on UserRepository and JwtService. Show me proper mocking.

prompt_templates.title: Configure TypeORM Entities
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a User entity with relationships to Posts and Comments. Show me how to configure OneToMany and ManyToOne relationships in TypeORM with NestJS.

output_examples.input: How do I fix 'Cannot resolve dependencies of UserService' error?
output_examples.output: This error typically means a provider is missing from your module. Check: 1) Is the dependency in the module's providers array? 2) Is it exported if used across modules? 3) Check for typos in provider names. Use forwardRef() if you have circular dependencies between two services.

output_examples.input: Set up JWT authentication with Passport
output_examples.output: 1) Install @nestjs/passport, @nestjs/jwt, passport, passport-jwt. 2) Create JwtStrategy extending PassportStrategy(Strategy). 3) Configure JwtModule with secret in forRoot(). 4) Create AuthGuard using JwtAuthGuard. 5) Apply @UseGuards(AuthGuard) to protected routes.

best_practices: Use dependency injection to keep services loosely coupled and testable
Follow the modular architecture: one feature module per domain entity
Always validate DTOs using class-validator and class-transformer pipes
anti_patterns: Avoid importing modules directly into controllers - use service injection
Do not skip validation pipes - always validate incoming request data
Avoid tight coupling between modules - use exports and interfaces

faq.question: What is NestJS?
faq.answer: NestJS is a Node.js framework for building efficient, scalable server-side applications. It uses TypeScript and combines elements of OOP, FP, and FRP.

faq.question: How do I fix circular dependencies in NestJS?
faq.answer: Use forwardRef() on both sides of the circular dependency, or refactor to extract shared logic into a third module.

faq.question: How do I test NestJS services?
faq.answer: Use @nestjs/testing to create Test.createTestingModule(). Mock dependencies using useValue or useClass, then compile and test the service.

faq.question: What is the difference between guards and interceptors?
faq.answer: Guards determine if a route can be accessed (authorization). Interceptors transform requests/responses or add extra logic before/after method execution.

faq.question: How do I configure TypeORM with NestJS?
faq.answer: Use @nestjs/typeorm package. Import TypeOrmModule.forRoot() in AppModule and TypeOrmModule.forFeature() in feature modules with entity arrays.

faq.question: Can this skill write complete applications?
faq.answer: This skill provides expert guidance, code patterns, and troubleshooting. It helps you understand and implement NestJS solutions rather than generating complete applications.
